constraints.name: Torsion bar OD valid (13.90mm)
constraints.detail: OD 13.90mm not in discrete garage options

constraints.name: RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: BMW: keep FARB at blade 1 (maximum front mechanical grip). Use RARB as the only live balance variable.
Rear ARB 'Soft' provides the correct blade range. If blade runs out of range, change ARB diameter — not FARB.
Stiffer RARB -> shifts load transfer rear -> front GAINS grip via LLTD -> sharpens front-end bite. Softer RARB -> stable/planted rear.
Cold tyre out-lap: RARB at blade 1 to prevent snap oversteer before tyres are up to temperature.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

constraints.name: Front conditioning to op temp
constraints.note: Vision tread: >20 laps indicates tyres may not reach temp in a stint

notes: Representative cornering: 3.43g (p95=2.13g, peak=3.99g, kerb_weight=0.7). Roll: 3.0° at representative, 3.5° at peak.
Tyre temperature spread diagnosis: inner hotter = correct camber. If outer runs hotter -> reduce negative camber by 0.2-0.3 deg.
BMW Vision tread conditioning (Sebring): fronts +2.4°C/lap, rears +3.2°C/lap. Full operating temp by lap 13-15 (fronts), 8-9 (rears).
For sprint qualifying: add 0.3° more negative camber + 0.2mm extra toe-out to accelerate thermal buildup.

constraints.note: Explicit click materialization recomputed from selected clicks.

constraints.name: Rear HS damping ratio
constraints.note: Explicit click materialization recomputed from selected clicks.

constraints.name: Front HS rbd > HS comp
constraints.note: Rebound must exceed comp to prevent wheel bounce.

constraints.name: Rear HS comp < Front HS comp
constraints.note: Compliance hierarchy: rear yields to bumps more than front.

notes: Front critical damping: 7502 N*s/m (modal k=50.0 N/mm, f_n = 2.12 Hz)
Rear critical damping: 13958 N*s/m (modal k=155.2 N/mm, f_n = 3.54 Hz)
Front LS: zeta=0.88 -> c=6602 N*s/m -> F@25mm/s = 165 N -> 9 clicks
Front HS: zeta=0.45 -> c=3376 N*s/m -> F@155mm/s = 522 N -> 7 clicks
Rear HS: zeta=0.21 -> c=2931 N*s/m -> F@185mm/s = 543 N -> 7 clicks
HS ref velocities: front p95=154.6mm/s, rear p95=185.3mm/s (rear +20% more active)
Damping ratios are derived from quarter-car eigenvalue analysis, NOT from empirical baseline matching.
Explicit damper materialization recomputed axle coefficients and damping ratios from selected clicks.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 46.0% | Fuel correction: -0.05% at 87L | Result: 46.0%; +0.8% from trail brake depth p95=0.98; +0.2% for high braking decel p95=2.07g; -0.5% for front braking lock proxy p95=0.108; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=26.3 m/s^2; Note: measured hydraulic split 79.1% vs recommended 46.3% (delta +32.8%); MC: F/R = 20.6/22.2 mm (ratio 0.93); differs from nominal 1.00 — effective bias may need decrease; Pad: Medium — low brake temps, softer compound may improve initial bite | front-lock evidence shifted hardware seed rearward
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, migration, master cylinders, and pad compound were conservatively seeded from braking evidence.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 27.2 Nm (lat transfer=13615N at peak lat_g=3.99g); +5 Nm for rear power slip p95=0.087; clutch plates=6 | Coast 45 deg (from trail brake depth=0.24); Drive 70 deg (from throttle R^2=0.65) | Lock: coast=61.2% drive=26.2% (preload 6.1% + plates 55.1%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; -1 gain for consistent driver; +1 slip for rear power slip p95=0.087; +1 gain: TC barely active (0%) but rear slip p95=0.087; +1 slip for high MGU-K torque 546 Nm
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 35°C → cold adj -1.5 kPa; Front hot 155 kPa → cold 152 kPa; Rear hot 156 kPa → cold 152 kPa
